session.assistant:
Wait — the comment body might not have an `objective-roadmap` metadata block. Let me check if the comment body typically contains one. Let me look at the test fixture `V2_COMMENT`:
Looking at `V2_COMMENT` in the test file, the comment has `erk:metadata-block:objective-body` not `erk:metadata-block:objective-roadmap`. So the comment may not always have a roadmap YAML block. I need to handle the case where the comment doesn't have an `objective-roadmap` block — in that case, `replace_metadata_block_in_body` would raise or do nothing. Let me check what happens:
session.tool_result:
670→def replace_metadata_block_in_body( 671→ body: str, 672→ key: str, 673→ new_block_content: str, 674→) -> str: 675→ """Replace a metadata block in the body with new content. 676→ 677→ Uses the HTML comment markers to locate and replace the block. 678→ This is used internally by update functions to replace individual blocks. 679→ 680→ Args: 681→ body: Full issue body 682→ key: Metadata block key (e.g., "plan-header") 683→ new_block_content: New rendered block content (from render_metadata_block()) 684→ 685→ Returns: 686→ Updated body with block replaced 687→ 688→ Raises: 689→ ValueError: If block not found 690→ """ 691→ # Pattern to match the entire metadata block from opening to closing comment. 692→ # Supports both closing tag formats: 693→ # - <!-- /erk:metadata-block:key --> 694→ # - <!-- /erk:metadata-block --> 695→ escaped_key = re.escape(key) 696→ pattern = ( 697→ rf"(?:<!-- WARNING: Machine-generated\. Manual edits may break erk tooling\. -->\n)*" 698→ rf"<!-- erk:metadata-block:{escaped_key} -->" 699→ rf"(.+?)"
... [5 lines omitted] ...
session.assistant:
It raises `ValueError` if block not found. But per the plan's context, the "objective-body comment has two data representations: rendered tables and a YAML block". Let me check if the comment actually has an `objective-roadmap` block. Looking at the test comment `V2_COMMENT`, it only has `objective-body` — not `objective-roadmap`.
But the plan says line 83: "also replace the `erk:metadata-block:objective-roadmap` block in the comment body". This implies some comments DO have this block. I should handle the case where the block doesn't exist in the comment gracefully (skip it):
